rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-aryl acrylamides bearing 2-aryl indoles followed by oxidative indole cleavage to give CF3-containing isoquinolinediones
electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2Na (Langlois reagent) to generate CF3 radical (loss of SO2, e-); subsequent intermediates are further oxidized anodically through several SET steps, with cathode reducing H+/H2O to H2 and regenerating mediator

key_intermediates.name: CF3 radical from anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na
alkyl radical A' after CF3 addition to acrylamide alkene
spirocyclic indolyl radical B' after radical cyclization onto indole C3
indolenium cation C' after anodic oxidation of B'
radical cation D' from further anodic SET
hydroxylated adduct E' from H2O addition to radical cation
dihydroxy intermediate F'/G' en route to C-C cleavage
ring-opened diketo/ortho-acyl amide product 5 (3a) after oxidative C-C cleavage of indole

electron_flow: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na releases CF3 radical, which adds to the acrylamide alkene; the resulting C-radical cyclizes onto the 2-aryl indole to give a spiro indolinyl radical, which undergoes sequential anodic SET oxidations and H2O additions to install two oxygens at the indole C2-C3 bond, ultimately cleaving the indole ring to the isoquinolinedione product. The cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a formed in 0%; TEMPO-CF3 adduct detected by HRMS ([M+H]+ calc 226.1408, found 226.1427), supporting CF3 radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: HRMS of product from reaction in H2-18O shows incorporation of one and two 18O atoms (m/z 468.0851 mono-18O and 470.0893 di-18O alongside 466.0818 unlabeled), confirming both carbonyl oxygens of cleavage product derive from water

probe_reagents.name: Time-course / reaction profile of 1a
probe_reagents.observation: 3aa' accumulates early (up to ~47% at 4 h) then decays as 3a grows, indicating 3aa' is an on-pathway intermediate that is further oxidized/cleaved to 3a

notes: Mechanism proposed for difluoroalkylation variant in Figure S6 is drawn analogously (CF2H instead of CF3) and invokes a Mn(II)/Mn(III) mediator in one cycle branch; alternative would be direct anodic SET without mediator. Spiro intermediate 3aa' is competent and converts to 3a, supporting an indole oxidative C-C cleavage by sequential water addition/SET rather than a single dioxygenation event.
